document: Photosynthesis converts light energy into chemical energy. Chlorophyll absorbs light. Light-dependent reactions make ATP and NADPH and release oxygen. The Calvin cycle uses ATP and NADPH to help form carbohydrates from carbon dioxide.
expected_summary: Photosynthesis captures light energy and stores it as chemical energy. Its light-dependent reactions produce ATP and NADPH and release oxygen, while the Calvin cycle uses those products to fix carbon into carbohydrates.

document: Water changes among solid, liquid, and gas. Melting turns solid into liquid, evaporation turns liquid into gas, condensation turns gas into liquid, and freezing turns liquid into solid.
expected_summary: Matter changes state through melting, evaporation, condensation, and freezing.
